summary: The current app writes the current-user Resume setting HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CrossDeviceResume\Configuration\IsResumeAllowed = 0. Repo notes and local registry observations confirm that this runtime user setting is real and behaves as expected on this build.

setting.name: Disable Resume for Current User
setting.casual_explanation: Resume lets you start on one device and continue on this PC. This tweak turns off that current-user Resume setting.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it off when they do not want cross-device handoff behavior for their account. They leave it on when they use Continue on PC or related app handoff flows.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces cross-device convenience for the current user. The app now matches the observed current-user setting rather than a separate managed policy surface.
setting.professional_notes: The current app writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CrossDeviceResume\Configuration\IsResumeAllowed = 0. Repo notes show Settings > Apps > Resume writing IsResumeAllowed = 0 when Resume is off and IsResumeAllowed = 1 when Resume is on. Microsoft also documents a separate user-scope policy CSP for managed deployment, but that is not the control surface this tweak uses.

allowed_values.label: No explicit user setting
allowed_values.meaning: Windows uses its normal current-user Resume behavior.

allowed_values.label: Resume disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Observed Settings and app value for turning the current-user Resume setting off.

allowed_values.label: Resume enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Observed Settings value for turning the current-user Resume setting on.

targets.notes: This is the runtime user setting the app currently writes.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes IsResumeAllowed = 0 under the runtime HKCU CrossDeviceResume configuration key.

windows_defaults.label: Windows managed default
windows_defaults.applies_to: User profiles where the current-user Resume setting is not explicitly set

states.rationale: Leave the current-user Resume setting unset.

windows_defaults.notes: This record now tracks the observed current-user Resume setting directly.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable Resume
intended_for: General users who do not want cross-device handoff
People who want Resume turned off for the current account
avoid_for: People who use Continue on PC or related cross-device handoff flows

states.rationale: Set the current-user Resume setting to off.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to disable Resume for the current account.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want cross-device handoff to remain available.
tradeoffs: Resume is disabled for the current user.
The setting is reversible by setting IsResumeAllowed back to 1.

evidence.title: Repo Procmon notes for Resume settings
evidence.summary: Repo notes show Settings > Apps > Resume writing IsResumeAllowed = 0 when Resume is off and IsResumeAllowed = 1 when Resume is on.

evidence.title: Local CrossDeviceResume registry observation
evidence.summary: Local read-only registry query on March 14, 2026 found IsResumeAllowed = 0 and IsOneDriveResumeAllowed = 1 under the current-user CrossDeviceResume configuration key, confirming the observed runtime path is present and active on this build.

evidence.title: Guest Procmon and PolicyManager probe for CrossDeviceResume
evidence.summary: Guest-side Procmon capture shows CrossDeviceResume.exe launching while PolicyManager DLL loads. The companion registry probe confirms the managed policy default branch at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Connectivity\DisableCrossDeviceResume with value 0 and no current-user PolicyManager Connectivity path on this build.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CrossDeviceResume\Configuration\IsResumeAllowed = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Guest Procmon probe for CrossDeviceResume resume toggle
evidence.summary: Guest-side reversible probe toggled IsResumeAllowed between 0 and 1 while opening the CrossDeviceResume settings surface. Procmon captured Explorer, ShellHost, SearchHost, and StartMenuExperienceHost activity around the toggle.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Policy CSP - Connectivity / DisableCrossDeviceResume
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents a separate managed policy surface for cross-device resume. This record keeps that policy as related context, but the app uses the runtime current-user setting instead.

decision.why: Repo notes and local registry observations confirm the current-user Resume setting, and the app now matches that observed surface directly.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Settings > Apps > Resume writing IsResumeAllowed = 0 when Resume is off and IsResumeAllowed = 1 when Resume is on.
validation_proof.notes: This record now documents the current-user Resume setting that the app actually writes. The companion guest Procmon probe captured CrossDeviceResume settings activity while IsResumeAllowed was toggled between 0 and 1 and then restored. The separate policy CSP remains related context, not the active control surface for this tweak.
